abstract: The object for specifying formatted text content and styling for captions in table cells.
overview:
## Discussion

Use a `FormattedText` object to provide the content and style for text strings you use in data tables and as caption descriptors. You can use a `FormattedText` object for:

To apply a style to a `FormattedText` object, you can use a [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ComponentTextStyle]. You can also use either HTML or [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/InlineTextStyle] objects to further customize ranges of text. See [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/using-html-with-apple-news-format].

You can use this object in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/DataDescriptor], [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/RecordStore], and [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/CaptionDescriptor].

### Example
rawMarkdown: --- source: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/FormattedText crawled: 2026-03-10T14:19:53Z --- # FormattedText **Object** The object for specifying formatted text content and styling for captions in table cells. ## Declaration ```swift object FormattedText ``` ## Discussion Use a `FormattedText` object to provide the content and style for text strings you use in data tables and as caption descriptors. You can use a `FormattedText` object for: - Row and column headers (the value of the `label` property in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/DataDescriptor]) - Data in JSON tables (the value of a key-value pair in a record in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/RecordStore]) To apply a style to a `FormattedText` object, you can use a [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ComponentTextStyle]. You can also use either HTML or [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/InlineTextStyle] objects to further customize ranges of text. See [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/using-html-with-apple-news-format]. You can use this object in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/DataDescriptor], [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/RecordStore], and [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/CaptionDescriptor]. ### Example ```json { "components": [ { "role": "datatable", "data": { "descriptors": [ { "identifier": "id-title", "key": "title", "dataType": "text", "label": "Title" } ], "records": [ { "title": { "type": "formatted_text", "text": "Name", "format": "html" } } ] } } ] } ``` ## Table Styles - **Defining and Using Table Styles**: Apply table styles, such as borders and backgrounds, to the rows, columns, and cells in your HTML and JSON tables. - **TableStyle**: The object for defining a style for rows, columns, cells, and headers in a table. - **TableRowStyle**: The object for applying styles to rows in a table. - **ConditionalTableRowStyle**: The object for applying styles to table rows that meet certain conditions. - **TableRowSelector**: The object for defining conditions that apply a conditional style to a row. - **TableColumnStyle**: The object for applying styles to columns in a table. - **ConditionalTableColumnStyle**: The object for applying styles to table columns that meet certain conditions. - **TableColumnSelector**: The object for defining conditions that apply a conditional style to a column. - **TableCellStyle**: The object for applying styles to cells in a table. - **ConditionalTableCellStyle**: The object for applying a style to table cells that meet certain conditions. - **TableCellSelector**: The object for defining conditions that apply a conditional style to a cell. - **TableBorder**: The object for setting borders for tables. - **TableStrokeStyle**: The object for defining the color, width, and style of a stroke in a table. - **Padding**: The object for defining space around the content in a table cell.
